response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a broad web, mobile, and embedded background, but has no demonstrable experience in SoC or IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. His low-latency work at Dunnhumby and embedded systems exposure are the closest relevant signals, but they are far removed from the principal-level hardware performance engineering this role requires. The gap between his profile and the job's core requirements is fundamental, not bridgeable by adjacent experience.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency systems programming using async I/O, fibers, and high-performance databases at Dunnhumby, showing some performance-oriented engineering instinct
Long, diverse career (17+ years) across embedded, backend, and systems software indicating adaptability and technical depth
Hands-on experience with performance profiling and optimization in distributed micro-services handling millions of requests per second
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, the primary technical requirement of the role
No background in AI/ML hardware, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload analysis — the core domain of this position
Educational background (two Bachelor's degrees in CS and Fine Arts) without hardware engineering focus does not align with the research-depth expected at Principal IC level for this domain

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
